inputs.label: Total Repair Orders (monthly)
inputs.businessContext: Total number of repair orders completed in the period (ISO 9001:2015 clause 8.5.1)

inputs.label: Comeback Count (monthly)
inputs.businessContext: Number of repair orders that returned for rework within warranty or service period (Six Sigma DPMO metric)

inputs.label: Average Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Fully burdened labor rate including overhead (WERC DC Measures)

inputs.label: Average Hours per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Average technician hours spent diagnosing and re-repairing a comeback (Lean Takt time impact)

inputs.label: Average Parts Cost per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Replacement parts and materials consumed per comeback (ISO 14001 waste reduction)

inputs.label: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated net profit from a loyal customer over their relationship with the shop

inputs.label: Lost Customer Rate per Comeback
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of customers who never return after a comeback experience (Net Promoter Score correlation)

inputs.label: Shop Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of repair facility influences overhead multipliers (ASA industry standards)

inputs.label: Include Hidden Costs (Goodwill, Admin, Downtime)
inputs.businessContext: When enabled, adds 20% overhead for scheduling disruption, customer goodwill gestures, and administrative rework (Lean Muda identification)

rules.message: Comeback count cannot exceed total repair orders.
Average hours per comeback must be greater than zero.
Customer lifetime value must be positive.
Lost customer rate must be between 0 and 100 percent.

thresholds.message: Comeback rate exceeds 10% — immediate process improvement required (Six Sigma DPMO > 100,000).
Comeback rate between 5% and 10% — investigate root causes (ISO 9001 corrective action).
Estimated lost future revenue exceeds $10,000 — customer retention strategy needed.

formulas.name: Comeback Rate
formulas.description: Percentage of repair orders that result in a comeback (DPMO base metric).

formulas.name: Direct Labor Cost of Comebacks
formulas.description: Total labor cost incurred for rework hours.

formulas.name: Direct Parts Cost of Comebacks
formulas.description: Total parts cost consumed for rework.

formulas.name: Hidden Overhead Cost
formulas.description: Additional costs from scheduling disruption, customer goodwill, and administrative overhead (20% of direct costs if enabled).

formulas.name: Lost Future Revenue
formulas.description: Estimated revenue lost from customers who defect after a comeback.

formulas.name: Shop Type Overhead Multiplier
formulas.description: Adjusts total cost based on shop type (dealer/franchise have higher overhead).
formulas.formula: CASE shop_type WHEN 'dealer' THEN 1.25 WHEN 'franchise' THEN 1.15 WHEN 'fleet' THEN 1.05 ELSE 1.0 END

formulas.name: Total Comeback Cost (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Comprehensive cost of comebacks including direct, hidden, and lost future revenue, adjusted by shop type.

primary.label: Total Comeback Cost
primary.description: Aggregate monthly cost of repair comebacks including direct, hidden, and lost future revenue, adjusted by shop type.

direct_labor_cost.label: Direct Labor Cost
direct_labor_cost.description: Total labor cost for rework hours.

hidden_overhead_cost.label: Hidden Overhead Cost
hidden_overhead_cost.description: Additional costs from scheduling disruption, goodwill, admin (if enabled).

lost_future_revenue.label: Lost Future Revenue
lost_future_revenue.description: Estimated revenue lost from customer defection.

shop_type_multiplier.label: Shop Type Multiplier
shop_type_multiplier.description: Overhead adjustment factor based on shop type.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Comeback Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Percentage of orders that are comebacks — primary quality indicator.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Lost Customer Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Percentage of customers lost per comeback — impacts long-term revenue.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Average Hours per Comeback
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Technician hours per comeback — reflects process inefficiency.

suggestedActions.label: Implement 5-Why Root Cause Analysis
suggestedActions.description: For every comeback, conduct a 5-Why analysis to identify systemic issues (Lean Six Sigma).
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduce comeback rate by 20-40% within 3 months.

suggestedActions.label: Standardize Repair Procedures
suggestedActions.description: Create standardized work instructions for top 10 recurring repairs (ISO 9001:2015 clause 7.5).
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Decrease average hours per comeback by 0.5 hours.

suggestedActions.label: Customer Follow-Up Program
suggestedActions.description: Call every customer 48 hours after repair to ensure satisfaction and catch issues early.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduce lost customer rate by 5 percentage points.

suggestedActions.label: Technician Certification on Complex Repairs
suggestedActions.description: Invest in ASE or manufacturer-specific training for high-comeback areas.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Lower parts cost per comeback by 10% and improve first-time fix rate.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Total Cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total cost adjusted by a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2) based on input completeness and variability. Default 1.0 if all inputs provided.
